---
title: "CV"
description: "Public CV for Miguel Cosenza Contreras."
---

<!-- TODO: Add a private PDF CV later if desired, after manual privacy review. -->

## Miguel Cosenza Contreras

Proteomics data scientist  
Freiburg im Breisgau, Germany  
[migueljcc5@gmail.com](mailto:migueljcc5@gmail.com) | [GitHub](https://github.com/MiguelCos) | [ORCID](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-9727-3229) | [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=DbsUEGsAAAAJ&hl) | [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/miguel-cosenza-contreras-972414114/)

## Profile

Proteomics data scientist with over six years of experience in clinical and experimental research. Trained as a biologist, I work at the interface of experimental design and data analysis, with a focus on mass-spectrometry-based proteomics for protein interaction research, cancer, cardiac disease, and infection biology.

At the University of Freiburg, within SFB1381 - Dynamic Organization of Cellular Protein Machineries and the Laboratory of Protease Degradomics, I build and maintain bioinformatic pipelines for studying protein interactions and proteolytic activity. My work includes complexome and blue-native mass spectrometry data analysis workflows, co-immunoprecipitation proteomics, and the integrative analysis of proteomics, terminomics, lipidomics, and proteogenomics data.

## Current Position

**Postdoctoral researcher / Bioinformatician**  
Laboratory of Protease Degradomics and SFB1381 - Dynamic Organization of Cellular Protein Machineries, Faculty of Biology, University of Freiburg  
Supervisor/PI: Prof. Dr. Pitter Huesgen  
April 2024-present

Evaluation, establishment, and application of bioinformatic pipelines for studying protein interactions and proteolytic activity. Work includes complexome profiling analysis, co-immunoprecipitation assays, blue-native gel mass spectrometry experiments, and shotgun mass-spectrometry-based proteomics workflows.

## Employment

**Doctoral researcher**  
Laboratory of Translational Proteomics, Institute of Surgical Pathology, University Medical Center Freiburg  
Supervisor/PI: Prof. Dr. Oliver Schilling  
2019-2024

**Research Assistant**  
Laboratory of Enzymology and Proteomics, Universidade Federal de Ouro Preto, Brazil  
Supervisor/PI: Prof. Dr. William de Castro Borges  
2019

**Lecturer, Biology Department**  
Universidad de Carabobo, Valencia, Venezuela  
August 2016-February 2017

Contracted part-time lecturer and biotechnology laboratory facilitator for biology students.

**Lecturer, Biology Department**  
Universidad de Carabobo, Valencia, Venezuela  
February 2016-July 2016

Contracted full-time lecturer for biochemistry and microbiology courses for biology students.

## Education

**PhD in Biology**  
University of Freiburg / University Medical Center Freiburg, 2019-2024  
Focus: proteomics data science, bioinformatics, and multi-omics data integration.  
Dissertation: *Proteomics data science and multi-omics data integration for enhanced exploratory insights of disease mechanisms.*  
Grade: summa cum laude.

**MSc in Biological Sciences**  
Federal University of Ouro Preto, Brazil, 2017-2019  
Focus: structural biochemistry and molecular biology.  
Project: Proteomics analysis of spleen during parasitic infection.  
GPA: 3.78/4.0; 91%.

**BSc in Biology**  
University of Carabobo, Venezuela, 2009-2015  
Five-year program. 2nd in promotion.

## Selected Publications

- Cosenza-Contreras M. et al. 2024. TermineR: Extracting information on endogenous proteolytic processing from shotgun proteomics data. *Proteomics* 24(19). [DOI: 10.1002/pmic.202300491](https://doi.org/10.1002/pmic.202300491).
- Cosenza-Contreras M. et al. 2024. Proteometabolomics of initial and recurrent glioblastoma highlights an increased immune cell signature with altered lipid metabolism. *Neuro-Oncology* 26(3):488-502. [DOI: 10.1093/neuonc/noad208](https://doi.org/10.1093/neuonc/noad208).
- Cosenza-Contreras M. et al. 2018/2019. The Schistosomiasis SpleenOME: Unveiling the Proteomic Landscape of Splenomegaly Using Label-Free Mass Spectrometry. *Frontiers in Immunology* 9:3137. [DOI: 10.3389/fimmu.2018.03137](https://doi.org/10.3389/fimmu.2018.03137).
- Kocsmar E., Schmid M., Cosenza-Contreras M. et al. 2023. Proteome alterations in human autopsy tissues in relation to time after death. *Cellular and Molecular Life Sciences* 80:117. [DOI: 10.1007/s00018-023-04754-3](https://doi.org/10.1007/s00018-023-04754-3).

## Awards

- PAEC OAS-GCUB scholarship for MSc, 2017-2019.
- Travel fellowship, International Proteolysis Society Conference 2023, Singapore.
- Travel fellowship, ISCB-LA-SoIBio-EMBNet Joint Bioinformatics Conference 2018, CABANA Project and Global Challenges Research Fund.

## Skills

- R, Bioconductor, Quarto/Markdown, R package development, Git/GitHub, VS Code, RStudio, and scientific workflow organization.
- Data mining, exploratory statistics, data modeling, machine learning, data visualization, and reproducible reporting.
- Integrative analysis of proteomics, terminomics, lipidomics, proteogenomics, and clinical multi-omics data.
- Mass-spectrometry-based proteomics, including complexome profiling, blue-native MS data analysis workflows, co-immunoprecipitation proteomics, experimental design, sample preparation, TMT and label-free quantification, and proteogenomics.
- Analysis workflows for HPLC-ESI-MS/MS, Q Exactive, timsTOF, FragPipe, DIA-NN, Spectronaut, MaxQuant/MSstats-style outputs, and related proteomics data structures.

## Languages

- Spanish: native.
- English: fluent.
- Portuguese: fluent.
- German: basic/limited working proficiency (B1).
